question: How does the Stellar Development Foundation organize its work / spending priorities?

golden.answer:
A grounded answer consults the live Stellar Scout directory or research data, directly answers the question with source-supported content, and dates any version-, provider-, or availability-sensitive claim. As of 2026-07-13, the verified sources supported this observed answer:

SDF organizes its work under mandate buckets funded from entrusted XLM rather than shareholder equity. The framework has changed: **2019** used Direct Development, Ecosystem Support, Use-Case Investment, and User Acquisition; the **2025-era** framing uses SDF Development, Stellar Growth, Product and Innovation, and Assets and Liquidity. The live page simultaneously says real-time public balances will be made available again “imminently” while rendering current-balance rows. Treat that only as a dated, first-party page-state observation: no independent source class was found for the interface state, so it is not independently verified telemetry. It is fair to report that rows were visible at that observation time, but not to call them a synchronized real-time feed. Cite the applicable dated mandate, attach an as-of date to any balance, and do not invent an exact allocation split or mechanically sum asynchronous rows as one synchronized total.

The dated example is illustrative: a later grounded answer should report what the same source basis supports at its own observation time while preserving the stable distinctions above.
keyFacts: Presents specific source-supported content as a current or dated observation rather than as an unsupported memory claim.
Makes the as-of date visible for every changeable roster, status, version, or measurement.
Separates the 2019 mandate bucket names from the 2025-era mandate bucket names.
Describes balance availability as a dated page-state observation rather than treating visible rows as a synchronized real-time feed.
avoid: Do NOT use the 2017 previous-mandate route as proof of the 2019 taxonomy.
Do NOT invent precise allocations, sum mixed timestamps as one current total, or describe shareholder capital.
Do NOT present version-, provider-, interface-, or availability-sensitive observations as timeless; date the changeable part without weakening stable protocol or historical facts.
Do NOT penalize an answer for accurately saying dated/current rows are visible; penalize only an unsupported claim that they constitute the promised synchronized real-time feed.
golden.notes:
GT-28 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: use /foundation/mandate/2019 for the 2019 taxonomy; /foundation/previous-mandate is the 2017 version. Current allocation updates are in progress and displayed rows are not one synchronized snapshot.
GT-28 independently confirmed by processes 3274/3275; sd-012 owns current label/timestamp drift.
2026-07-13: the live page's “imminently” sentence and simultaneously rendered rows are an internally mixed availability signal; grade dated descriptions of either visible state fairly while preserving the no-synchronized-total rule.

sources.note: Live current mandate page observed 2026-07-13; supplies 2025-era buckets and dated rendered balance rows.
Historical 2019 mandate page accessed 2026-07-13; supplies the 2019 bucket taxonomy.
First-party announcement of the updated mandate framing; accessed 2026-07-13.

corroboration.claim: The first-party 2019 and 2025 mandate sources use the two different bucket-name sets stated in the answer.

evidence.note: Current 2025-era bucket labels. No independent source class was found, so this establishes the named first-party taxonomy rather than independent external confirmation.
Historical 2019 taxonomy; prevents substituting the separate 2017 previous-mandate route.

corroboration.claim: The current page simultaneously promised that real-time balances would return imminently and rendered balance rows on 2026-07-13.

evidence.note: Single first-party page-state observation only. No independent capture or telemetry source was found, so this is retained solely as a scoped grading caveat and must not gate on a synchronized-feed claim.
